enrichment.short_description: Open-source network source-of-truth platform for DCIM and IPAM, with optional hosted NetBox Cloud and self-hosted NetBox Enterprise.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: NetBox is an open-source (Apache 2.0) network source-of-truth for DCIM and IPAM, with free self-hosting and paid NetBox Cloud/Enterprise options.

features.title: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM)
features.description: Model racks, devices, cables, and physical/virtual assets in a central database.

features.title: IP address management (IPAM)
features.description: Track IP addresses, VLANs, VRFs, and circuits alongside network device data.

features.title: Network automation source of truth
features.description: Serves as the authoritative data source that automation tools query for structured network state.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: A large ecosystem of community and vendor plugins extends NetBox's core data model.

features.title: Audit logging
features.description: Records changes made within the platform for traceability.

features.title: REST/GraphQL access and integrations
features.description: Exposes network data for automation workflows and third-party tooling.

pros: Core NetBox is free, open source (Apache 2.0), and self-hostable
Widely adopted as a network source of truth (21k+ GitHub stars)
Large plugin ecosystem extends core functionality
Offers a managed NetBox Cloud option for teams that don't want to self-host
cons: Advanced capabilities (NetBox Enterprise, Cloud, Discovery, Assurance) are paid products from NetBox Labs
Public pricing for NetBox Cloud/Enterprise is not published on the marketing pages
Self-hosting the open-source edition requires your own infrastructure and maintenance

faqs.question: Is NetBox free?
faqs.answer: The core NetBox platform is open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to self-host.

faqs.question: What is NetBox Cloud?
faqs.answer: NetBox Cloud is a fully-hosted SaaS version of NetBox offered by NetBox Labs.

faqs.question: Is there a self-hosted paid option?
faqs.answer: Yes, NetBox Enterprise is a self-hosted, commercially supported edition backed by NetBox Labs.

faqs.question: Where is the source code?
faqs.answer: NetBox is developed publicly at github.com/netbox-community/netbox.

use_cases.title: Network documentation
use_cases.description: Maintain an authoritative record of racks, devices, cabling, and IP space.

use_cases.title: Network automation source of truth
use_cases.description: Feed validated network data into automation and configuration pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is NetBox?
paragraphs: NetBox is an open-source network source-of-truth platform used to model and document data center infrastructure (DCIM) and IP address space (IPAM). It stores network device, rack, cabling, IP, VLAN, and circuit data in a central database that automation tools can query.

The core project is Apache 2.0 licensed and free to self-host from github.com/netbox-community/netbox. NetBox Labs also offers commercially hosted (NetBox Cloud) and self-hosted, supported (NetBox Enterprise) editions on top of the open-source core.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who NetBox is for
paragraphs: NetBox suits network engineers and infrastructure teams that need a structured, queryable record of their network as the foundation for automation, rather than spreadsheets or disconnected tools.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source Network Source of Truth and automation platform from Network to Code, with a free Community Edition and paid Professional/Enterprise/Cloud tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nautobot is an open-source network source-of-truth and automation platform with a free Community Edition and paid enterprise/cloud tiers from Network to Code.

features.title: Inventory management
features.description: Tracks devices, IPs, circuits, and VLANs in a centralized data model.

features.title: Multi-vendor support
features.description: Works across network equipment from vendors including Cisco, Arista, and Juniper.

features.title: Dynamic configuration generation
features.description: Generates device configurations from validated network data.

features.title: REST and GraphQL APIs
features.description: Exposes network data for custom automation workflows.

features.title: Python-based Jobs system
features.description: Runs Python jobs inside Nautobot for task automation.

features.title: Custom apps and webhooks
features.description: Extensible through custom applications and webhook integrations.

pros: Community Edition is open source and free to self-manage
Supports multi-vendor network environments
Backed by Network to Code with professional support, training, and managed-service options
Extensible via Python jobs, custom apps, and APIs
cons: Professional, Enterprise, and Cloud editions with additional features are paid and quote-based
Specific pricing is not published; requires contacting Network to Code
Self-managed Community Edition requires your own hosting and maintenance

faqs.question: Is Nautobot free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is open source and free to self-host and manage.

faqs.question: Who develops Nautobot?
faqs.answer: Nautobot is developed by Network to Code, a company founded in 2014 and headquartered in New York City.

faqs.question: Are paid editions available?
faqs.answer: Yes, Network to Code offers Professional, Enterprise, and fully managed Cloud editions with additional features and support.

faqs.question: Does Nautobot support automation?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a Python-based Jobs system and REST/GraphQL APIs for building automation workflows.

use_cases.title: Network source of truth
use_cases.description: Centralize device, IP, circuit, and VLAN data across a multi-vendor network.

use_cases.title: Network automation platform
use_cases.description: Drive configuration generation and validation from a single data source.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nautobot?
paragraphs: Nautobot is an open-source Network Source of Truth and Automation Platform that helps teams unify network data, model intended state, and drive automation. It supports multi-vendor inventory management, dynamic configuration generation, and REST/GraphQL APIs.

It is developed by Network to Code, a company founded in 2014 and based in New York City. Nautobot offers a free, self-managed Community Edition alongside paid Professional, Enterprise, and fully managed Cloud editions with additional support and features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nautobot is for
paragraphs: Nautobot fits network engineering teams managing multi-vendor infrastructure who need a source of truth to drive network automation, with an option to scale into paid support and managed services as needs grow.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source IP address management tool with IPv4/IPv6 tracking, VLAN/VRF support, and a REST API.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: phpIPAM is a free, open-source (GPLv3) IP address management tool with IPv4/IPv6, VLAN/VRF support, a REST API, and Docker deployment.

features.title: IPv4/IPv6 address management
features.description: Tracks and organizes IP address space with VLAN and VRF support.

features.title: Subnet management
features.description: Displays automatic free space calculation across subnets.

features.title: Domain authentication
features.description: Supports Active Directory, LDAP, and RADIUS authentication.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Exposes IPAM data for integration with other systems.

features.title: PowerDNS integration
features.description: Integrates with PowerDNS for DNS record management.

features.title: Device and rack management
features.description: Tracks devices and rack placement alongside IP data.

features.title: IP request module
features.description: Provides a workflow for requesting IP addresses, plus custom fields.

pros: Completely free and open source (GPLv3) with no licensing costs
Actively maintained on GitHub with regular releases
REST API and PowerDNS integration support automation workflows
Supports Docker deployment and multiple domain authentication methods
cons: Requires self-hosting on your own server infrastructure
No official managed/hosted offering or paid support plan is advertised
No native mobile apps

faqs.question: Is phpIPAM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, phpIPAM is free, open-source software released under the GPLv3 license.

faqs.question: Does phpIPAM have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a REST API for integrating IPAM data with other tools.

faqs.question: Can phpIPAM be deployed with Docker?
faqs.answer: Yes, Docker deployment is supported and documented.

faqs.question: Does phpIPAM support IPv6?
faqs.answer: Yes, it manages both IPv4 and IPv6 address space.

use_cases.title: IP address management
use_cases.description: Track and organize IPv4/IPv6 subnets, VLANs, and VRFs for a network.

use_cases.title: DNS and network automation
use_cases.description: Integrate IPAM data with PowerDNS and other tooling via the REST API.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is phpIPAM?
paragraphs: phpIPAM is a free, open-source IP address management tool that tracks IPv4 and IPv6 address space, VLANs, and VRFs. It includes subnet management with automatic free-space display, domain authentication (AD/LDAP/RADIUS), a REST API, and PowerDNS integration.
The project is released under the GPLv3 license and is self-hosted, with installation guides for common Linux distributions and Docker deployment support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who phpIPAM is for
paragraphs: phpIPAM suits network administrators and sysadmins who want a free, self-hosted IPAM tool with API access for integrating IP data into other systems.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source datacenter asset management system for tracking hardware, rack space, and network configuration.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RackTables is a free, open-source (GPLv2) datacenter asset management tool for tracking hardware, rack space, and network addresses.

features.title: Hardware asset documentation
features.description: Documents servers and other hardware assets in a datacenter or server room.

features.title: Rack space allocation
features.description: Tracks physical rack space and equipment placement.

features.title: IP address tracking
features.description: Records network addresses associated with tracked assets.

features.title: Network configuration documentation
features.description: Captures network setup details tied to hardware assets.

pros: Completely free and open source (GPLv2)
Self-hostable with no licensing costs
Actively maintained on GitHub with a public bug tracker and roadmap
Purpose-built for datacenter/server room asset tracking
cons: Requires self-hosting and your own server infrastructure
No official managed hosting or commercial support plan advertised
Interface and feature set are dated compared to modern commercial CMDB tools

faqs.question: Is RackTables free?
faqs.answer: Yes, RackTables is free, open-source software released under GPLv2.

faqs.question: What does RackTables track?
faqs.answer: It tracks datacenter hardware, IP addresses, rack space allocation, and network configuration details.

faqs.question: Where is the source code?
faqs.answer: RackTables is developed on GitHub at github.com/RackTables/racktables, with a mirror at git.racktables.org.

faqs.question: What was the latest stable version?
faqs.answer: The latest stable version referenced on the project site is 0.22.0.

use_cases.title: Datacenter asset tracking
use_cases.description: Document servers, rack space, and hardware inventory for a datacenter or server room.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RackTables?
paragraphs: RackTables is a free, open-source datacenter asset management system used to document hardware, network addresses, physical rack space, and network configuration. It is released under the GNU General Public License v2.
The project is self-hosted and maintained on GitHub, with a public bug tracker and roadmap. The latest stable version referenced on the project site is 0.22.0.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RackTables is for
paragraphs: RackTables suits infrastructure and datacenter administrators who need a free, self-hosted way to document physical hardware, rack layout, and associated network details.

enrichment.short_description: IT infrastructure discovery, CMDB, and dependency-mapping platform, now part of Freshworks, priced as an annual subscription by device count.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Device42 is an IT infrastructure discovery, CMDB, and dependency-mapping platform, now part of Freshworks, with a free trial and device-based annual pricing.

features.title: Infrastructure discovery
features.description: Automated discovery spanning legacy systems, mainframes, and cloud containers.

features.title: CMDB
features.description: A configuration management database with near real-time, automated updates.

features.title: Dependency mapping
features.description: Visualizes relationships and dependencies between assets and applications.

features.title: Cloud discovery
features.description: Discovers and inventories AWS and Azure resources.

features.title: Storage management
features.description: Provides visibility into storage resources.

features.title: IPAM
features.description: Centralized IP address management.

features.title: Software license management
features.description: Automated license discovery and compliance tracking.

pros: Broad discovery coverage from legacy/mainframe systems to cloud infrastructure
Combines CMDB, dependency mapping, IPAM, and license management in one platform
Free trial available before purchase
Backed by Freshworks after acquisition
cons: Pricing is quote-based and not published publicly
Priced by number of devices, which can scale cost with infrastructure size
Now part of Freshworks following acquisition, which may affect standalone roadmap

faqs.question: Is there a free trial of Device42?
faqs.answer: Yes, Device42 offers a downloadable free trial.

faqs.question: How is Device42 priced?
faqs.answer: It is sold as an annual subscription based on the number of devices, with pricing available on request.

faqs.question: Who owns Device42?
faqs.answer: Device42 has been acquired by Freshworks.

faqs.question: Does Device42 support cloud discovery?
faqs.answer: Yes, it discovers and inventories resources in AWS and Azure.

use_cases.title: IT asset discovery and CMDB
use_cases.description: Automatically discover and maintain an up-to-date inventory of infrastructure assets and their dependencies.

use_cases.title: Cloud migration planning
use_cases.description: Map application dependencies before migrating workloads to the cloud.
use_cases.audience: IT operations and cloud migration teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Device42?
paragraphs: Device42 is an IT infrastructure discovery, asset management, and dependency-mapping platform that provides visibility across hybrid IT environments, from legacy and mainframe systems to cloud infrastructure. It includes a CMDB, cloud discovery for AWS and Azure, storage management, IPAM, and software license management.

Device42 is headquartered in San Mateo, California, and has been acquired by Freshworks. It is sold as an annual subscription priced by device count, with a free trial available and quote-based pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Device42 is for
paragraphs: Device42 fits IT operations teams that need automated discovery and an up-to-date CMDB across hybrid infrastructure, including cloud migration and compliance use cases.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered ITSM platform from Freshworks with incident, asset, and workflow management, starting at $19/agent/month billed annually.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Freshservice is Freshworks' ITSM platform with AI-assisted incident, asset, and workflow management, from $19/agent/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Freddy AI Copilot and Insights
features.description: AI features for instant issue resolution and analytics, available on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Incident management
features.description: Proactive monitoring and incident response workflows.

features.title: Asset management and CMDB
features.description: Connected asset data providing context for faster ticket resolution.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Low-code orchestration, intelligent routing, and service catalogs.

features.title: Unified service delivery
features.description: Integrates ITSM, IT asset management, and IT operations management on one platform.

features.title: Employee onboarding workflows
features.description: Cross-departmental coordination across IT, HR, and Finance.

pros: Clear, published per-agent pricing across four tiers
14-day free trial available before purchase
Combines ITSM, asset management, and workflow automation in one platform
Backed by Freshworks with a large existing customer base
cons: Freddy AI Copilot is a paid add-on ($29/agent/month) even on paid plans
Advanced modules (problem, change, major incident management) require the higher Pro tier
Enterprise tier pricing is custom/quote-based

faqs.question: How much does Freshservice cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $19/agent/month (Starter) billed annually, up to $99/agent/month (Pro); Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Freshservice offers a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does Freshservice include AI features?
faqs.answer: It includes Freddy AI capabilities, with the Freddy AI Copilot available as a $29/agent/month add-on.

faqs.question: Who makes Freshservice?
faqs.answer: Freshservice is developed by Freshworks Inc.

use_cases.title: IT service management
use_cases.description: Handle incidents, service requests, and change management with ITIL-aligned workflows.

use_cases.title: MSP service delivery
use_cases.description: Manage service desk operations for managed service provider clients.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Freshservice?
paragraphs: Freshservice is an ITSM platform from Freshworks built on ITIL practices, covering incident, asset, and workflow management alongside AI-assisted features branded Freddy AI. It offers four pricing tiers, from Starter ($19/agent/month) to a custom-priced Enterprise plan, all billed annually.
A 14-day free trial is available. Higher tiers add capabilities such as problem management, change management, major incident management, and additional Freddy AI features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Freshservice is for
paragraphs: Freshservice fits IT teams and MSPs that want a published, per-agent-priced ITSM platform with a trial period, scaling from basic help desk needs to full ITIL-aligned service management.

enrichment.short_description: No-code ITSM platform with embedded AI, offering a fixed-price Starter plan from $1,499/year and per-agent Pro/Enterprise tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: InvGate Service Desk is a no-code ITSM platform with AI features, priced from $1,499/year with a free trial on Starter and Pro plans.

features.title: AI ticket summarization
features.description: Uses AI to highlight critical information in tickets.

features.title: Virtual Service Agent
features.description: AI agent operating within Microsoft Teams, WhatsApp, and Slack.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Visual workflow builder with pre-built templates.

features.title: Self-service portal and knowledge management
features.description: Self-service portal with AI-assisted knowledge article generation from resolved tickets.

features.title: Change and problem management
features.description: Supports ITIL-aligned change and problem management processes.

features.title: Analytics dashboards
features.description: Over 150 built-in metrics with customizable dashboards.

features.title: Asset inventory integration
features.description: Links tickets to IT Asset Inventory for unified asset-ticket tracking.

pros: Published pricing for Starter and Pro plans
Free trial available on Starter and Pro plans
No-code workflow builder with pre-built templates
AI Service Agent integrates with Teams, WhatsApp, and Slack
cons: Enterprise plan requires custom quote starting at $12,000/year
Pro plan pricing scales per agent ($500/agent/year) on top of a base cost
Vendor notes prices may change without prior notice

faqs.question: How much does InvGate Service Desk cost?
faqs.answer: Starter is $1,499/year fixed, Pro starts at $2,500/year ($500/agent/year for up to 50 agents), and Enterprise starts at $12,000/year with custom pricing.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available for the Starter and Pro plans.

faqs.question: Does InvGate Service Desk include AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI ticket summarization and a Virtual Service Agent via its AI Hub.

faqs.question: Can Enterprise be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan includes on-premise hosting as an option.

use_cases.title: IT service desk
use_cases.description: Manage incidents, requests, and workflows with a no-code builder.

use_cases.title: Multi-departmental service management
use_cases.description: Extend ITIL-aligned workflows to non-IT departments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is InvGate Service Desk?
paragraphs: InvGate Service Desk is an ITSM platform combining a no-code workflow builder with embedded AI features, including ticket summarization and a Virtual Service Agent that works within Microsoft Teams, WhatsApp, and Slack.

Pricing is published for two of its three tiers: Starter at a fixed $1,499/year, and Pro starting at $2,500/year ($500/agent/year for up to 50 agents). The Enterprise tier is custom-priced, starting at $12,000/year and including on-premise hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who InvGate Service Desk is for
paragraphs: InvGate Service Desk suits IT and service teams that want transparent starting pricing and a no-code workflow builder, scaling from small teams on Starter to enterprise deployments with on-premise hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Unified ITSM platform with all-inclusive per-agent pricing (from £66/agent/month), covering incident, change, problem, and asset management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HaloITSM is a unified ITSM platform with all-inclusive per-agent pricing from £66/agent/month and a 30-day free trial, no credit card required.

features.title: Incident and major incident management
features.description: Ticket routing, SLA tracking, and escalation workflows.

features.title: Service request management
features.description: Self-service portal with forms and automation.

features.title: Change management
features.description: Approval workflows and impact assessment for changes.

features.title: Problem management
features.description: Root cause analysis and known error documentation.

features.title: Asset and CMDB
features.description: Configuration tracking and service dependency mapping.

features.title: Halo AI
features.description: Built-in automation for categorization, summarization, and suggested resolutions.

features.title: 1,000+ built-in reports
features.description: Out-of-the-box reporting and analytics.

pros: All-inclusive licensing model bundles core features into one price with no feature-gated tiers
30-day free trial with all features, no credit card required
Flexible deployment: Halo Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Premise
ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certified with a 99.99% uptime commitment
cons: Per-agent pricing varies by region, complicating cost comparison
Only internal agents are licensed; end users/customers are free but the agent-based model can still scale in cost for larger support teams
Enterprise unlimited-license package is a large fixed $1M/year commitment

faqs.question: How much does HaloITSM cost?
faqs.answer: Standard UK pricing is £66 per agent per month, billed annually; pricing varies by region.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial with all features is available and no credit card is required.

faqs.question: What deployment options are available?
faqs.answer: Halo Cloud, Private Cloud, and On-Premise deployments are all supported.

faqs.question: Are end users charged?
faqs.answer: No, end users and customers who access the portal are always free; only agents are licensed.

use_cases.title: Unified ITSM
use_cases.description: Run incident, request, change, problem, and asset management from one licensed platform.

use_cases.title: MSP service delivery
use_cases.description: Deliver ITSM across multiple clients with concurrent or named licensing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HaloITSM?
paragraphs: HaloITSM is a unified IT service management platform from Halo Service Solutions covering incident, request, change, problem, and asset management. It uses an all-inclusive licensing model, bundling core ITSM functionality into a single per-agent price rather than charging separately for modules.

Standard UK pricing is £66/agent/month billed annually, with regional pricing elsewhere and a 30-day free trial that includes all features with no credit card required. Deployment options include Halo Cloud, Private Cloud, and On-Premise.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HaloITSM is for
paragraphs: HaloITSM suits enterprises and MSPs that want a single, all-inclusive ITSM price without paying extra for individual modules, and that need flexible deployment options.

enrichment.short_description: AI-driven ITSM and asset management platform from ManageEngine (Zoho), with a free 5-technician edition and paid plans from $13/technician/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ServiceDesk Plus is ManageEngine's ITSM and asset management platform, free for up to 5 technicians, with paid plans from $13/technician/month.

features.title: AI ticket triage (Zia)
features.description: Predictive intelligence and a virtual support agent for ticket handling.

features.title: GenAI-powered responses
features.description: Integrates with ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot for AI-generated replies.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Automates ticket handling, SLA management, and escalations without code.

features.title: Self-service portal and knowledge base
features.description: Lets end users submit and track requests and search knowledge articles.

features.title: CMDB and IT asset management
features.description: Available on Professional and Enterprise editions.

features.title: Multi-instance model
features.description: Separate instances with data segregation for different departments.

features.title: On-premises and cloud deployment
features.description: Available as both an on-premises and cloud-hosted deployment.

pros: Free edition available for up to 5 technicians
Published per-technician pricing across Standard, Professional, and Enterprise editions
AI/ML capabilities included at no extra cost across all editions
Certified for 14 ITIL practices
cons: Free edition is limited to 5 technicians and one service desk instance
IT asset management features require the Professional or Enterprise edition
Pricing scales with both technician count and asset count on paid tiers

faqs.question: Is ServiceDesk Plus free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free edition is available for up to 5 technicians.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Standard starts at $13/technician/month, Professional at $27/technician/month, and Enterprise at $67/technician/month.

faqs.question: Is it available on-premises?
faqs.answer: Yes, both on-premises and cloud deployment options are offered.

faqs.question: Who makes ServiceDesk Plus?
faqs.answer: It is developed by ManageEngine, part of the Zoho ecosystem.

use_cases.title: IT help desk
use_cases.description: Handle incident and request management for IT teams.

use_cases.title: Enterprise service management
use_cases.description: Extend service management workflows to HR, facilities, and finance departments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus?
paragraphs: ServiceDesk Plus is an AI-driven ITSM platform from ManageEngine (part of Zoho) that combines IT service management, IT asset management, and CMDB capabilities, and extends into enterprise service management for departments like HR and finance.

It offers a free edition limited to 5 technicians on the Standard tier, plus paid Standard, Professional, and Enterprise editions starting at $13, $27, and $67 per technician per month respectively, available both on-premises and in the cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ServiceDesk Plus is for
paragraphs: ServiceDesk Plus fits small IT teams that can start on the free 5-technician edition, as well as larger enterprises needing asset management, change management, and multi-department service delivery on paid editions.

enrichment.short_description: Commercial service management platform for customer service, ITSM, and ISMS from OTRS AG; the free Community Edition was discontinued in 2020.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OTRS is a commercial service management platform for customer service, ITSM, and ISMS from OTRS AG, with quote-based pricing and managed hosting.

features.title: Ticketing and service desk
features.description: Core ticketing functionality for customer service and IT support.

features.title: Business process management
features.description: Workflow automation for service processes.

features.title: Asset/device management and CMDB
features.description: Tracks assets and configuration items.

features.title: Reporting and KPI measurement
features.description: Built-in reporting and key performance indicator tracking.

features.title: AI-powered summaries
features.description: AI-generated ticket summaries and analytics.

features.title: Kanban views and time tracking
features.description: Visual workflow boards and time tracking for tickets.

pros: Covers customer service, ITSM, ISMS, and cyber defense use cases in one platform
Includes AI-powered summaries and analytics
Multilingual support (English, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Hungarian, French, Italian)
Managed/hosted deployment offered by OTRS AG
cons: The free ((OTRS)) Community Edition was discontinued (EOL) at the end of 2020, so new users get only the commercial product
No public pricing; requires contacting sales for a demo and quote
Organizations still on the legacy Community Edition rely on community forks (e.g., Znuny) rather than official OTRS AG support

faqs.question: Is OTRS still open source?
faqs.answer: The free ((OTRS)) Community Edition was discontinued at the end of 2020; OTRS is now offered as a commercial, managed product by OTRS AG.

faqs.question: What alternatives exist for the old Community Edition?
faqs.answer: Community forks such as Znuny continue to provide free, open-source alternatives based on the last Community Edition release.

faqs.question: What is OTRS used for?
faqs.answer: It is used for customer service, IT service management (ITSM), information security management (ISMS), and cyber defense.

faqs.question: Is pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, OTRS pricing is not published; prospects must contact OTRS AG for a demo and quote.

use_cases.title: IT service management
use_cases.description: Manage tickets, changes, and CMDB data for IT teams.

use_cases.title: Customer service and ISMS
use_cases.description: Run customer service ticketing alongside information security management processes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OTRS?
paragraphs: OTRS is a service management platform from OTRS AG used for customer service, IT service management (ITSM), information security management (ISMS), and cyber defense. It includes ticketing, business process management, asset/CMDB tracking, reporting, and AI-powered ticket summaries.

The historically free ((OTRS)) Community Edition was declared end of life at the end of 2020, and OTRS AG now promotes migrating to its commercial, managed OTRS offering. Organizations that want a free, open-source alternative typically use community forks such as Znuny instead.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OTRS is for
paragraphs: OTRS fits organizations wanting a commercially supported, managed service management platform spanning ITSM, customer service, and ISMS, rather than teams looking for a free self-hosted ticketing system.
